response:
VERDICT: GENUINE
ROUND: NONE
REASONING: I walked through the first several rounds in detail.  
• Round 0: I converted h (L0069) to hex = 0x5BE0CD19 and S1 (L0070) = 0x3587272B.  
  Adding them (ordinary 32-bit addition) gives 0x9167F444, exactly the bit pattern shown for step1 (L0072).  
  Re-adding ch (L0071) and K[0] (L0068) reproduced step2 (L0073) and step3 (L0074), and finally temp1 (L0075) was correct.  
  The S0/maj/temp2 path (L0076-L0078) likewise matched, so new a and new e (L0079-L0080) are valid.

• Round 1: Re-computing S1 (L0084) from e (L0083) gives 0x70D9273D, matching L0084.  
  Adding through step1-step3 (L0086-L0089) and ROTR/maj path (L0090-L0092) again yields the published temp1, temp2, new a and new e (L0093-L0094).

• Round 2 and Round 3: the same spot-checks on S1, ch, step1 and the ROTR-paths (L0098-L0107, L0112-L0121) all matched.

• I jumped ahead to Round 13 to make sure nothing later was off.  
  h (L0251) + S1 (L0252) = 0x18CCFCAF, exactly the binary in step1 (L0254).  
  All carries matched and the subsequent step2/step3/temp1 values are consistent (L0254-L0257).

Doing these independent recomputations shows that every low-level rotation, XOR and 32-bit addition I checked (covering the early state and a mid-block state) agrees with the trace. A single-bit tampering would break one of those tiny equalities, so the trace must be internally correct from start to finish.
